key_insight: Charged atomic planes in SmNiO3 buffer layers can deterministically set the polarization direction of ultrathin BaTiO3 films and enable net ferroelectric polarization from the first unit cell.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical interface-engineering route to integrate reliable, single-unit-cell ferroelectric layers into nanoscale devices by suppressing the usual critical-thickness limit.
method: Use (001)-oriented SmNiO3 buffer layers with controlled surface termination
Select [SmO]+ or [NiO2]- terminations to orient BaTiO3 polarization upward or downward
Combine experiments with DFT to assess how oxygen vacancies affect interfacial polarization
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that net polarization appears in BaTiO3 films starting from the first deposited unit cell, with polarity controlled by the chosen SNO termination.
watch_item: The polarizing effect is sensitive to defect chemistry, so oxygen vacancies at the interface can weaken or counteract the intended polarization control.
paper_title: Polarizing ultrathin ferroelectric BaTiO3 films through interfacial layer polarization
